content:
# Basic SoccerTwos Tactical Notes

## Defensive rotation
When one player pressures the ball, the second player should maintain recovery positioning rather than overcommit.

## Counterattack pattern
A transition chance is strongest when possession changes in midfield and the far-side player immediately supports forward.

## Common failure pattern
Double-committing near the ball often leaves the goal exposed after a failed challenge.

## Safer shape
If the ball is contested near the wall, one player should challenge while the second stays central enough to protect the goal.
Blue won after better late-game defensive recovery and cleaner counterattacks.
Step 45 | Team orange | Type pressure | Tag press | Orange forced Blue deep with early high pressure.
Step 120 | Team blue | Type goal | Tag counterattack | Blue scored after a transition from midfield.
Step 180 | Team blue | Type recovery | Tag defensive_shape | Blue recovered into shape and blocked a central lane.
Step 240 | Team orange | Type goal | Tag sustained_pressure | Orange equalized after sustained pressure.
Step 320 | Team blue | Type chance | Tag transition | Blue created a high-value transition chance from a loose ball.
Step 410 | Team orange | Type mistake | Tag double_commit | Orange double-committed and left the goal exposed.
Step 430 | Team blue | Type goal | Tag punish_mistake | Blue capitalized immediately on the defensive gap.
Step 470 | Team blue | Type recovery | Tag game_management | Blue slowed the game and protected the lead with safer shape.
